Appendix 3
Toxic plants and substances
Nicola Bates
Poisoning in guinea pigs is not commonly reported but  Dermal decontamination
those cases that are reported usually involve substances  Dermal decontamination may be required to remove sub-
found in the guinea pig’s environment, such as plants and  stances on the skin or hair. For most substances, use of a

Vitamin K1 should be administered

mg/kg i.m. as needed depending

any evidence of further bleeding
